reasoning: The valuation is genuinely cheap and the pivot to higher-margin credit/alternatives is encouraging. But ₪29B in AUM outflows is a structural concern — you can't grow forever by shifting to higher-margin products while your core business shrinks. At 4.3% below cost and 9.7% of portfolio, monitor the AUM trend closely. ENTRY/EXIT CONDITIONS: Add if AUM stabilizes (quarterly data shows flat or positive flows). Exit if AUM outflows accelerate beyond ₪40B/quarter.
entryConditions: Add if AUM stabilizes (quarterly data shows flat or positive flows)
exitConditions: Exit if AUM outflows accelerate beyond ₪40B/quarter.
bullCase: Altshuler Shaham delivered a strong 2025 — net income ₪128M to shareholders (+13.3%), driven by high-margin credit (+453%) and alternatives (+103%) businesses. P/E ~10x is cheap vs sector avg 14-16x. Margins expanding (13.7% vs 12.2%). The pivot to credit and alternatives is working.
bearCase: Core AUM saw ₪29B outflows — the asset management business is shrinking despite profitability growth. Weak 3-year fund performance. Stock dropped 45% from peak before recovering. V-shaped recovery may be fragile. Single Israeli financial stock with geopolitical exposure.
